extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Adam attacked with 6.1km to go on Jebel Hafeet, dropped EVENEPOEL in final kilometers to win stage while EVENEPOEL defended 9-second GC lead.
tactical_patterns: AG2R placed HANNAN in breakaway as positioning marker for BOUCHARD's late move, securing fourth place.
YATES attacked at 6.1km remaining rather than earlier, suggesting steeper upper ramps suit his explosive power profile better.
GROUPAMA placed both BELOVUS and HESSMAN in break to support STORER, indicating they anticipated team depth mattering on climb.
PLAPP defended podium by limiting losses rather than attacking, one-second margin over YATES shows conservative tactics nearly failed.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: explosive on steeper ramps at 6.1km, dropped EVENEPOEL despite latter defending GC, 10-second winning margin reveals superior climbing
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: defended 9-second lead despite being dropped by YATES, statement ride suggests controlled loss rather than collapse
rider_signals.PLAPP Lucas: neutral: battled to hold podium by one second over YATES, defensive ride nearly cost him, shows vulnerability on sustained steep gradients
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: negative: lost podium by three seconds to PLAPP, couldn't distance GC rivals despite pre-stage expectations
rider_signals.BOUCHARD Geoffrey: positive: AG2R breakaway strategy with HANNAN delivered fourth place, tactical execution reveals team understanding of his limitations
rider_signals.LIZENECA: negative: pre-stage favorite disappeared on climb, complete absence from decisive action reveals poor form
